title: 1.1: Tests that get_metrics() returns an aggregated result containing all expected metric types from a complete API response
1.2: Tests that get_metrics() returns records for both master and standby database nodes for each metric
1.3: Tests that get_metric() returns a metric object with metric_name matching the requested metric type
1.4: Tests that get_metric() returns records for both database roles when extracting a single metric
1.5: Tests that get_metric() falls back to previous data point when latest value is null
1.6: Tests that records are excluded when null values appear in multiple consecutive positions
2.1: Places the test for parsing all metrics in tests/monitor/test_upcloud_get_metric.py
2.2: Places the test for single metric extraction in tests/monitor/test_upcloud_get_metric.py
2.3: Places the tests for null value handling in tests/monitor/test_upcloud_get_metric.py
3.1: All tests use the pytest framework
3.2: All tests follow the test_<name> naming pattern
3.3: All tests use standard assert statements for verification
4.1: Follows JSON mock data pattern with cols and rows structure for test case that parses all metrics
